prompt:
A plastic bottle of wood glue with a tapered applicator tip stands on a workbench beside two clamped wooden boards, a thin bead of glue visible squeezed along the joint line. A small droplet is frozen at the tip, showing that it has just been used to bond the pieces together. The bottle’s label is simple and icon-based, with no readable text, making it clear that this is a dedicated adhesive for wood rather than a generic glue.

The scene is a near-future solarpunk woodworking space with soft, even lighting, clean walls, and organized clamps and tools hanging behind the bench. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or legible labels on the bottle, and a composition that focuses on the glue bottle and clamped boards while the rest of the shop remains softly out of focus, emphasizing careful, safe assembly work.
